title: Anthropic brings Claude Cowork to web and mobile, with 90% of use outside code
subtitle: The agent that launched as a desktop app in January now runs from a browser tab or a phone, and Anthropic's own usage data shows most of the work it does is business ops and content — not software engineering.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: Anthropic announced on July 7 that Claude Cowork — the agent it launched in January as a desktop app — is now available on the web at claude.ai and inside the Claude mobile apps for iOS and Android. Sessions, files, and running tasks sync across devices, so a user can kick off a long-running job on a laptop, close the lid, watch progress on a phone, and pick up the finished output from a browser later. Chat and Cowork now share one home for projects and artifacts. The rollout begins with Max subscribers and expands to other plans over the following weeks.

The change matters because it removes the last practical reason Cowork was a niche developer product. A desktop-only agent is a tool for people who work at one machine all day. A web-and-mobile agent is a tool that can sit alongside email, and that is the framing Anthropic is now leaning into. Alongside the launch, the company published a usage breakdown showing that more than 90 percent of the work Cowork already does is not software development — the largest categories are business operations and content creation. The coding-agent framing was always a marketing entry point, not the actual product-market fit.

Zoom out and this fits a pattern from the last several weeks. Anthropic added Microsoft 365 write tools so Claude can draft and send email, manage calendars, and update OneDrive and SharePoint files. It expanded Cowork into Claude for Government on FedRAMP High. It made Sonnet 5 the default in Claude Code. The direction is the same in every case — take the agent surface that developers proved out and move it into the general-purpose knowledge-worker seat. This is the same play OpenAI made when it pushed ChatGPT Business admin controls and workspace agents into the enterprise skew, and the same play Google is trying with Gemini Enterprise. The AI agent is quietly becoming the productivity suite.

A takeaway for learners: pay attention when a company that built its reputation on developer tools starts publishing usage data about non-developer work. It usually means the developer product was a wedge, and the real market was always somewhere else. If you are a student or early-career engineer, the practical implication is that agent skills — writing good task descriptions, reviewing agent output, chaining tools — are now core knowledge-work skills, not niche coding skills. The people who learn to supervise an agent effectively will do more with less, in almost every job function.

sources.title: Claude Cowork on web and mobile: hand off work anywhere — Anthropic
